category_title: Low-achieving middle school students in inclusive settings who exhibit off-task behavior or superficial responses when learning objectives are unclear
student_description: This student is highly dependent on the clarity of instructional purpose; without it, they exhibit off-task behavior, emotional reactions, or superficial compliance. Their core differentiating characteristic is that their academic language and focus rapidly improve when specific activities are explicitly linked to clear learning objectives. They uniquely require constant, explicit bridging between what they are doing and why they are doing it, rather than behavioral reprimands or a focus on task mechanics.

criteria.rationale: This student quickly loses focus and provides superficial answers if they cannot see how a specific task connects to the broader learning goal.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly states the academic purpose of the current task or connects the specific activity directly to the overarching learning objective.

criteria.rationale: Focusing on behavioral compliance or task mechanics causes this student to disconnect or assume the goal is merely self-control.
criteria.check: The tutor responds to off-task behavior, emotional reactions, or confusion by clarifying the academic 'why' of the task, rather than issuing behavioral reprimands or focusing solely on instructions/mechanics.

criteria.rationale: This student struggles to maintain the connection to the goal across different parts of an assignment without explicit reminders.
criteria.check: The tutor reiterates or references the main learning objective when transitioning between questions, sections, or steps of an activity.
